title: rosa wulfenii var. dolorosa
intro: Rosa wulfenii var. dolorosa, also known as the White Herb-Rose, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Rosaceae, characterized by its white flowers and fragrant, slightly bitter leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Rosa wulfenii var. dolorosa include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Rosa wulfenii var. dolorosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
